chapters.description: Create a product and price in your Stripe account.

nodes.description: Write code that creates a Stripe product with a price using the API. The product and price IDs should be used in subsequent steps — store them in a variable or constant in your code, NOT in .env files.
nodes.title: Create a product
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the product has a default price and that the product or price ID is stored in code for the Checkout Session step.
nodes.review_risk: If the price ID is missing or stale, Checkout can charge the wrong amount or fail later in the flow.

chapters.title: Set up your product catalog

chapters.description: Build a server endpoint that creates a Checkout Session and redirects the customer.

nodes.description: Create a Checkout Session with the price from the previous step.
nodes.title: Create a Checkout Session
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the Checkout Session uses the price created in product setup and has the intended success and cancel URLs.
nodes.review_risk: A mismatched price or redirect URL creates a payment flow that works technically but sends customers to the wrong purchase or page.

nodes.description: Add a button or link that redirects the customer to the Checkout page.
nodes.title: Redirect to Checkout
nodes.review_prompt: Open the app and confirm the checkout button redirects to Stripe Checkout instead of staying on a local route.
nodes.review_risk: If the button points at the wrong route, customers cannot reach the hosted Checkout flow even when the backend works.

nodes.description: Create a page to show after the customer completes payment.
nodes.title: Add a success page
nodes.review_prompt: Complete a test Checkout Session and confirm the success page renders after returning from Stripe.
nodes.review_risk: A broken return page makes completed payments look failed to customers and increases support burden.

chapters.title: Create a Checkout Session

chapters.description: Listen for webhook events to handle fulfillment.

nodes.description: Set up a webhook endpoint to receive the checkout.session.completed event and fulfill the order.
nodes.title: Handle checkout.session.completed
nodes.review_prompt: Run `stripe trigger checkout.session.completed` and confirm fulfillment happens only after verifying the Stripe signature.
nodes.review_risk: Webhook signature mistakes can fulfill orders from spoofed events or miss real completed payments.

chapters.title: Handle post-payment events

chapters.description: Verify the complete flow works end to end.

nodes.description: Run through the complete payment flow using a test card number.
nodes.title: Make a test payment
nodes.review_prompt: Run the full test-card payment flow and confirm it reaches Checkout, returns to success, and records fulfillment.
nodes.review_risk: The individual pieces can pass independently while the end-to-end customer path still fails.

chapters.title: Test the integration

description: Set up Checkout to accept a one-time payment with a prebuilt, hosted payment page.
title: Accept a one-time payment
